initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input grid
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Row and column indices (last row = N1, last col = M1)
maxRow(Rmax) :- Rmax = N-1.
maxCol(Cmax) :- Cmax = M-1.

% Colours that actually appear in the input
present(C) :- input(R, C2, V), V = C.

% Highest colour that appears  this will be the wall colour
maxColor(9) :- present(9).
maxColor(8) :- present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(7) :- present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(6) :- present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(5) :- present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(4) :- present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(3) :- present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(2) :- present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(1) :- present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(0) :- present(0), not present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).

% The wall colour  the highest colour that is present (nonzero)
wall(W) :- maxColor(W), W != 0.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Zero cells that lie on the border
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = maxRow.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = maxCol.

% Zero cells reachable from the border through other zero cells only
reachable(R,C) :- borderZero(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC, input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output cell definition
% 1. Cells that are the wall colour stay unchanged
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, Color = V, wall(W).

% 2. Zero cells that can reach the border stay 0
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, reachable(R,C), Color = 0.

% 3. Zero cells that are enclosed (not reachable) become the wall colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, not reachable(R,C), Color = W, wall(W).

% 4. All other nonzero nonwall cells keep their original colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != W, Color = V, wall(W).

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Color).

rounds.program_after:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input grid
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Row and column indices (last row = N1, last col = M1)
maxRow(Rmax) :- nRows(N), Rmax >= 0, Rmax < N.
maxCol(Cmax) :- nCols(M), Cmax >= 0, Cmax < M.

% Colours that actually appear in the input
present(C) :- input(R, C2, V), V = C.

% Highest colour that appears  this will be the wall colour
maxColor(9) :- present(9).
maxColor(8) :- present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(7) :- present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(6) :- present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(5) :- present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(4) :- present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(3) :- present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(2) :- present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(1) :- present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(0) :- present(0), not present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).

% The wall colour  the highest colour that is present (nonzero)
wall(W) :- maxColor(W), W != 0.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Zero cells that lie on the border
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = maxRow.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = maxCol.

% Zero cells reachable from the border through other zero cells only
reachable(R,C) :- borderZero(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC, input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output cell definition
% 1. Cells that are the wall colour stay unchanged
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, Color = V, wall(W).

% 2. Zero cells that can reach the border stay 0
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, reachable(R,C), Color = 0.

% 3. Zero cells that are enclosed (not reachable) become the wall colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, not reachable(R,C), Color = W, wall(W).

% 4. All other nonzero nonwall cells keep their original colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != W, Color = V, wall(W).

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Color).

% -------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input grid
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Row and column indices (last row = N1, last col = M1)
maxRow(Rmax) :- nRows(N), Rmax >= 0, Rmax < N.
maxCol(Cmax) :- nCols(M), Cmax >= 0, Cmax < M.

% Colours that actually appear in the input
present(C) :- input(R, C2, V), V = C.

% Highest colour that appears  this will be the wall colour
maxColor(9) :- present(9).
maxColor(8) :- present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(7) :- present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(6) :- present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(5) :- present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(4) :- present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(3) :- present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(2) :- present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(1) :- present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(0) :- present(0), not present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).

% The wall colour  the highest colour that is present (nonzero)
wall(W) :- maxColor(W), W != 0.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Zero cells that lie on the border
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = maxRow.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = maxCol.

% Zero cells reachable from the border through other zero cells only
reachable(R,C) :- borderZero(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC, input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output cell definition
% 1. Cells that are the wall colour stay unchanged
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, Color = V, wall(W).

% 2. Zero cells that can reach the border stay 0
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, reachable(R,C), Color = 0.

% 3. Zero cells that are enclosed (not reachable) become the wall colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, not reachable(R,C), Color = W, wall(W).

% 4. All other nonzero nonwall cells keep their original colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != W, Color = V, wall(W).

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Color).

final_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
% Dimensions of the input grid
numRows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
numCols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Row and column indices (last row = N1, last col = M1)
maxRow(Rmax) :- nRows(N), Rmax >= 0, Rmax < N.
maxCol(Cmax) :- nCols(M), Cmax >= 0, Cmax < M.

% Colours that actually appear in the input
present(C) :- input(R, C2, V), V = C.

% Highest colour that appears  this will be the wall colour
maxColor(9) :- present(9).
maxColor(8) :- present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(7) :- present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(6) :- present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(5) :- present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(4) :- present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(3) :- present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(2) :- present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(1) :- present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).
maxColor(0) :- present(0), not present(1), not present(2), not present(3), not present(4), not present(5), not present(6), not present(7), not present(8), not present(9).

% The wall colour  the highest colour that is present (nonzero)
wall(W) :- maxColor(W), W != 0.

% 4connected neighbourhood
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Zero cells that lie on the border
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, R = maxRow.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = 0.
borderZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, C = maxCol.

% Zero cells reachable from the border through other zero cells only
reachable(R,C) :- borderZero(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R = R1+DR, C = C1+DC, input(R,C,V), V = 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
% Output cell definition
% 1. Cells that are the wall colour stay unchanged
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = W, Color = V, wall(W).

% 2. Zero cells that can reach the border stay 0
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, reachable(R,C), Color = 0.

% 3. Zero cells that are enclosed (not reachable) become the wall colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V = 0, not reachable(R,C), Color = W, wall(W).

% 4. All other nonzero nonwall cells keep their original colour
output_cell(R,C,Color) :- input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != W, Color = V, wall(W).

% Exactly one output per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C,Color).
